info.name: JobLoom — User Management
info.description:
## User Management Module

Complete collection for all User Management endpoints in the JobLoom platform.

### Flows Covered
- **Registration Flow:** Register → Verify OTP → Authenticated
- **Login Flow:** Login → Get Token
- **Password Reset Flow:** Forgot Password → Verify OTP → Reset Password
- **Profile Management:** View, Update (with file uploads), Delete

### Authentication
Protected routes require a **JWT Bearer token**. Use the `Login` or `Verify Registration` request — the test scripts automatically save the token to `{{token}}`.

### Base URL
`http://localhost:3000/api`

### Environment Variables
| Variable | Description | Set By |
|---|---|---|
| `{{baseUrl}}` | API base URL | Manual |
| `{{token}}` | JWT auth token | Auto (Login / Verify Registration) |
| `{{userId}}` | Current user ID | Auto (Login / Verify Registration) |
| `{{resetToken}}` | Password reset token | Auto (Verify Password Reset) |

item.name: 1. Registration Flow
item.description:
Step-by-step flow to register and activate a new user account.

**Order:**
1. Register User → creates account, sends OTP to phone
2. Verify Registration OTP → activates account, returns JWT token

exec: pm.test('Account is unverified on registration', function () {
console.log('Check phone for OTP, then call Verify Registration.');

request.description:
Creates a new user account and sends a **6-digit OTP** to the provided phone number via SMS (Text.lk).

**Note:** Account is created with `isVerified: false`. Must call `Verify Registration` to activate.

**Roles:** `job_seeker` | `employer` | `admin`

**Required fields:** firstName, lastName, email, password (min 6 chars), role, phone, location.village, location.district, location.province

**On Success (201):** Returns user details. Token is NOT returned yet — verify OTP first.

Validates the 6-digit OTP sent to the user's phone during registration.

**OTP expires after 10 minutes.**

**On Success:** Account is activated (`isVerified: true`) and a JWT token is returned. The test script automatically saves `{{token}}` and `{{userId}}`.

item.name: 2. Login Flow
item.description: Authenticate with email and password to receive a JWT Bearer token.

request.description:
Authenticates a user with email and password.

**On Success:** Returns user details and a JWT Bearer token (valid for 7 days by default). The test script automatically saves `{{token}}` and `{{userId}}`.

**Use the token:** Add `Authorization: Bearer {{token}}` to any protected request header.

Login with an employer account. Saves token to `{{employerToken}}` and ID to `{{employerId}}` for use in job management requests.

**First register an employer account** (change role to `employer` in the Register request) then log in here.

item.name: 3. Password Reset Flow
item.description:
3-step flow to reset a forgotten password.

**Order:**
1. Forgot Password → sends OTP to phone
2. Verify Password Reset OTP → returns resetToken
3. Reset Password → sets new password using resetToken

request.description:
Sends a **6-digit OTP** to the user's registered phone via SMS.

**OTP expires after 10 minutes.**

Next step: call `Verify Password Reset OTP` with the OTP received.

response.name: 400 — Phone Not Found

request.description:
Validates the OTP received via SMS. Returns a secure `resetToken` required for the final password reset step.

**OTP expires after 10 minutes.**

The test script automatically saves the `resetToken` to `{{resetToken}}`.

Next step: call `Reset Password` with the saved token.

exec: console.log('Password reset successful. Reset token cleared.');

request.description:
Sets a new password using the `resetToken` from `Verify Password Reset OTP`.

**The new password is automatically hashed** by a Mongoose pre-save hook.

After success, the reset token is invalidated. The test script clears `{{resetToken}}` from the environment.

**Then:** Log in again with the new password.

item.name: 4. Profile Management
item.description:
Protected endpoints for viewing and managing the authenticated user's profile.

**All requests require:** `Authorization: Bearer {{token}}`

Run `Login` or `Verify Registration` first to populate `{{token}}`.

exec: pm.test('Response has user profile fields', function () {

request.description:
Returns the full profile of the currently authenticated user.

**Password is always excluded** from the response.

**Requires:** `Authorization: Bearer {{token}}`

variable.description: MongoDB ObjectId of the target user

request.description:
Fetches a user's profile by their MongoDB ObjectId.

**Password is always excluded** from the response.

**Requires:** `Authorization: Bearer {{token}}`

**Path Variable:** Replace `:id` with the user's MongoDB ObjectId, or use `{{userId}}` which is set automatically after login.

formdata.description: Optional — JSON string of location object
Optional — JSON string of skills array

formdata.value: [{"title": "Farm Supervisor", "company": "Green Fields Ltd", "duration": "2 years", "description": "Managed paddy cultivation"}]
formdata.description: Optional — JSON string of experience array

request.description:
Updates text-based profile fields. No file uploads in this variant.

**Content-Type:** `multipart/form-data` (auto-set by Postman for form-data body)

**All fields are optional.** Send only what you want to update.

**JSON string fields:** `location`, `skills`, `experience` must be sent as JSON strings.

**Returns:** Updated user + refreshed JWT token (auto-saved to `{{token}}` by test script).

formdata.description: Upload a profile photo (jpg, jpeg, png — max 5MB)

request.description:
Updates profile with a profile image upload.

**File:** Select a `jpg`, `jpeg`, or `png` file (max 5MB) for the `profileImage` field.

File is saved to `uploads/profiles/` on the server.

formdata.description: Upload a CV file (pdf, doc, docx — max 5MB, up to 5 files)

request.description:
Uploads one or more CV files to the user's profile.

**File field name:** `cv` (select multiple files to upload up to 5 at once)

**Allowed types:** pdf, doc, docx — max 5MB each

First uploaded CV is automatically set as primary if no CVs exist yet.

Files are saved to `uploads/cvs/` on the server.

formdata.description: The _id of the CV to promote as primary

request.description:
Promotes a specific CV to primary by its `_id`.

All other CVs will have `isPrimary` set to `false`.

**Replace** the `primaryCvId` value with the actual `_id` from the user's `cvs` array (visible in `Get My Profile` response).

formdata.description: The _id of the CV to remove

request.description:
Removes a CV from the user's profile by its `_id`.

**Replace** the `deleteCvId` value with the actual `_id` from the user's `cvs` array (visible in `Get My Profile` response).

Changes the authenticated user's password.

The new password is **automatically hashed** by the Mongoose pre-save hook before storage.

**Requires:** `Authorization: Bearer {{token}}`

item.name: 5. Account Deletion
item.description: Soft delete — deactivates the account (`isActive: false`) without permanently removing data.

exec: console.log('Account deleted. Token and userId cleared from environment.');

request.description:
Deactivates the authenticated user's account by setting `isActive: false`.

**This is a soft delete** — data is retained in the database.

**Requires:** Current password as confirmation to prevent unauthorized deletion.

The test script clears `{{token}}` and `{{userId}}` from the environment after successful deletion.

item.name: 6. Error Cases
item.description: Requests demonstrating common error responses for testing and validation.

request.description:
Demonstrates the validation error response when required fields are missing.

**Expected:** `400 Bad Request` with an `errors` array listing each missing field.

response.name: 400 — Validation Errors

request.description:
Demonstrates the 401 response when no Authorization header is provided.

**Expected:** `401 Unauthorized` with `{ message: 'Not authorized, no token' }`

Demonstrates the 401 response when an invalid or expired JWT token is provided.

**Expected:** `401 Unauthorized` with `{ message: 'Not authorized, token failed' }`
